codeReview.summary: Implementation covers UI structure and state machine for intro/active/concluded states but fails to display AI responses in chat. When user sends a message, the API call to add_message is made but the returned AI response is never added to the messages list or displayed in the UI. This is a blocking issue that prevents the chat from functioning as intended - user sees their own messages but Marcus Aurelius never responds.

issues.description: send_message() calls api_client.add_message() but never adds the AI response to self.messages. The result from add_message is checked but only to update session_state - the actual message content (AI response) is never extracted or displayed. User will send messages but never see Marcus respond.
issues.suggestedFix: After successful api_client.add_message(), extract the returned message data (AI response) and append it to self.messages list, then call _update_messages_list() to display it. The API should return the complete conversation state including AI response.

issues.description: send_message() calls app.page.update() before the API call completes. If the API call fails, message was already added to UI but then removed - this creates a race condition where user sees message briefly before it disappears. Additionally, the async nature means UI updates at wrong time.
issues.suggestedFix: Move app.page.update() to after the API response is handled, or use proper async flow control to ensure UI state matches backend state before updating.

issues.description: test_val_session_002_session_state_machine only verifies initial intro state, not the actual transition from intro to active. The comment says 'verify we're showing intro content' but this doesn't test the transition trigger.
issues.suggestedFix: Add test that simulates first message and verifies session_state becomes 'active' and content switches from intro to active chat.

issues.description: test_val_session_003_chat_messages uses wrapping and direct message append instead of actually testing the send_message async flow with API. The test doesn't verify actual message sending - it manually adds message then checks state.
issues.suggestedFix: Use AsyncMock to properly test the async send_message flow including API call and response handling.

fulfillmentCheck.VAL-SESSION-001: fail - Session creates with intro state, but there's no verification that intro card actually displays Marcus avatar with 'M' and 'Begin Conversation' button in a running Flet app. Unit tests mock the build and search controls, but this doesn't validate actual rendering.
fulfillmentCheck.VAL-SESSION-002: partial - State machine intro->active->concluded exists in code, but transition from active to concluded is not tested with actual API call. end_session() method exists but tests don't verify state actually changes.
fulfillmentCheck.VAL-SESSION-003: fail - User messages are added to UI, but AI responses are never displayed. The add_message API call is made but response content is not extracted or added to messages list.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The flet-worker skill procedure mentions 'Verify Implementation' with steps to run app, test navigation, verify forms. However, worker never actually ran 'flet run' to interactively verify the UI. This is documented in skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true but the actual UI behavior (AI responses not displaying) suggests verification was insufficient.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true, but only pytest tests were run - no interactive flet app verification. Tests mock create_task but don't test real async initialization flow.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker started backend API server but didn't document how to verify API endpoints work. Services.yaml has backend-api service but worker didn't verify the session endpoints actually return expected data format.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker created api_client methods but never did a live test against the actual API. The validation only used mocks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker spent time implementing session state machine but didn't discover/verify that AI responses from add_message API should be extracted and displayed. This is a critical gap - the code assumes response contains message data but this was never verified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: send_message() at line 291 checks 'if result:' but never extracts 'message' or 'response' from result to add to self.messages.

summary: Review of ui-session-chat feature finds blocking issue: chat UI shows user messages but never displays AI responses. The send_message() method calls add_message API but doesn't extract/display the returned response. Additionally, async flow has race conditions with app.page.update() called at wrong times. 23 unit tests pass but they use heavy mocking that didn't catch the missing AI response display. VAL-SESSION-003 fails because AI responses never appear in chat. Recommend fixing send_message to extract and display AI response, then verify with integration test against live API.
